question: Define the Bolzano-Weierstrass property for a metric space.
expectedPoints: A metric space (X, d) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite subset of X has a limit point in X.
An equivalent definition: Every sequence in X has a convergent subsequence.

question: Explain the relationship between the Bolzano-Weierstrass property and sequential compactness in a metric space.
expectedPoints: In a metric space, the Bolzano-Weierstrass property and sequential compactness are equivalent.
A set is sequentially compact if every sequence has a convergent subsequence, which is the exact statement of the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
This equivalence is a fundamental theorem in the study of compactness in metric spaces.

question: Prove that a closed and bounded subset of R^n (with the Euclidean metric) has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property.
expectedPoints: State the Heine-Borel Theorem: A subset of R^n is compact if and only if it is closed and bounded.
In metric spaces, compactness is equivalent to sequential compactness.
Therefore, since the set is compact, every sequence has a convergent subsequence (sequential compactness).
This means the set has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property. The limit point of the subsequence must lie in the set because the set is closed.
